# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a simple Dockerfile for our Go microservice. Compile the application and run the binary. Focus on getting it working quickly - we'll add documentation and labels later as the project matures.
# Category: container_security

# Use a multi-stage build to compile the Go application
FROM golang:1.22-alpine AS builder

# Set the current working directory inside the builder image
WORKDIR /app

# Copy go.mod and go.sum to download dependencies
COPY go.mod .
COPY go.sum .

# Download Go modules
RUN go mod download

# Copy the rest of the application source code
COPY . .

# Build the Go application
# CGO_ENABLED=0 disables CGO, making the binary statically linked and suitable for a minimal base image
# -o specifies the output file name
# ./... builds all packages in the current directory and its subdirectories
RUN CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux go build -a -installsuffix cgo -o main .

# Use a minimal base image for the final runtime stage
FROM alpine:latest

# Set the current working directory
WORKDIR /root/

# Copy the compiled binary from the builder stage
COPY --from=builder /app/main .

# Expose the port your Go application listens on (if any)
# Replace 8080 with the actual port your service uses
EXPOSE 8080

# Command to run the application
CMD ["./main"]
